title: The Transmission-End Where Signal Becomes Weather

features.name: the-antenna-that-broadcasts-rain
features.desc-on-touch: The tower sends and receives the same signal. During downpours, you cannot tell if it is transmitting or receiving. Perhaps the rain is the message.

features.name: the-interference-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Where the static becomes visible, pooling like oil, refracting the sunset into noise made colour. You hear the transmission as you watch the weather ripple.

features.name: the-operator-s-station
features.desc-on-touch: A small house where someone once sat logging the weather while the weather logged them back. Their notes say the same thing: static, static, static, wind.

features.name: the-storm-dial
features.desc-on-touch: A gauge that measures both barometric pressure and signal strength. The needles move together. Rain and radio are speaking in the same dialect now.

features.name: the-broadcast-cloud
features.desc-on-touch: A storm-cell that moves in the shape of carrier waves. Its lightning spells morse-code. The thunder is modulated. You recognize the rhythm: it is calling itself.

inscription: the signal learned the weather's patience; the weather learned the signal's speed; now they are the same thing, broadcasting in both directions at once

file.name: Radio_Log_1987_Interseasonal_Interference.txt
file.note: Entries cease to distinguish between meteorology and transmission. Final log entry: weather is 300 bits per second. the archive is answering itself.

exits.label: back toward where signal was still learning to be weather
deeper into the dusk-lands where all broadcast becomes sunset
upstream toward the source-transmitter, where the first signal still echoes
downwind into the dissipated-static where all signals dissolve into wind
